item.title: Here's what experts want you to know about using AI to help with your finances
item.description: "Hallucinations", privacy and other factors to consider if you're turning to artificial intelligence for financial advice.

item.title: Baby Reindeer creator Richard Gadd returns to TV with new series Half Man
item.description: After Baby Reindeer — about a comedian and his stalker — became a surprise hit, Richard Gadd ignored the pressure to replicate his success, making the show he couldn't stop thinking about.

item.title: Inside the vaults of Australia's legal MDMA and psilocybin magic mushroom trade
item.description: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

item.title: Missing man Jack McLennan's ex-girlfriend feared being 'jumped'
item.description: The former girlfriend of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan says she held fears for her safety because of rumours surrounding his disappearance.

item.title: Rate hikes and tax changes have broken the back of the latest property boom
item.description: Early signs of an Australian housing market correction are now clearly visible, and at the heart of the recent property market weakness is less vendor power.

item.title: BHP has pledged to hit net zero emissions by 2050. Inside the mining giant there are doubts
item.description: The mining giant's delay in the rollout of renewable energy and a fleet of electric trucks and trains in the Pilbara has raised doubts inside the company about it meeting its ambitious climate goal.

item.title: Labor consults on capital gains tax carve-outs as businesses call for 'reset'
item.description: The government is pushing ahead with capital gains tax changes while consulting on limited carve-outs.

item.title: What Xi served Putin, Trump and Albanese at China's state banquets reveals more than any diplomatic statement
item.description: Xi Jinping has learned that every ingredient placed on a state table is a word in a diplomatic sentence.

item.title: Neale Daniher's life as a leader helped him drive the fight against MND
item.description: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.

item.title: NACC chief Paul Brereton to face Senate grilling after shock resignation
item.description: National Anti-Corruption Commissioner Paul Brereton will face a grilling by senators at estimates hearings today following his shock resignation on Monday, amid calls for a wider reset of the troubled agency.

item.title: The sporting spectacle of the Enhanced Games was underwhelming but the financial rewards were not
item.description: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.

item.title: ISIS-linked woman under exclusion order not returning to Australia with other families
item.description: The majority of the final group of Australians living in a notorious Syrian camp for the families of killed and captured Islamic State militants are expected to arrive in Australia soon. Here's what we know about them.

item.title: Bridgewater Bridge engineer warns of foundation issues that builder disputed during design
item.description: Despite government assurances Hobart's Bridgewater Bridge is safe, there are fresh concerns over its foundation design, with one expert who worked on the project suggesting it could eventually lead to a need to reduce traffic flow or speed limits.

item.title: One year after Byron Haddow's Bali death his family demands action
item.description: A year after their 23-year-old son died in Bali in "unclear" circumstances, a Queensland couple have unanswered questions.

item.title: Cricket's next generation of pace knows the toll fast bowling can take
item.description: Cricket has few more glorious sights than a fast bowler terrorising a top-class batting order, but becoming a speedster is not without its pains, as the nation's next generation is finding out.

item.title: Woman charged with murder after child found dead in Lowood
item.description: Emergency services were called to a Lowood residence just before 11:10pm on Monday.

item.title: Former Berrivale workers remember Riverland food manufacturing history
item.description: Sixteen years after workers clocked off for the final time, Berrivale workers reunited to remember the cannery and preserving factory that employed generations of locals.

item.title: Thanasi Kokkinakis, Alex de Minaur and Daria Kasatkina advance to French Open second round
item.description: Thanasi Kokkinakis has roared back from the brink of defeat after four-and-a-quarter hours in sweltering Paris heat to launch another trademark five-set comeback at the French Open.

item.title: Pope Leo warns of AI becoming 'yet another Tower of Babel' in encyclical
item.description: Pope Leo XIV urges global governments to slow down the development of AI systems and their disruptive effects.

item.title: Bringing Them Home report inaction a 'failure', former commissioner says
item.description: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.

item.title: Angus Taylor pressures PM to detail small business CGT carve-outs in QT — as it happened
item.description: The opposition leader has pressured the prime minister to reveal details of small business carve-outs for capital gains tax discount changes.

item.title: Australian stocks fall as US strikes dampen investor sentiment — as it happened
item.description: The local share market is in the red, while shares in stock exchange operator ASX Ltd have tumbled on a trading update.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Teen star Eva Carneiro becomes Super Netball's youngest-ever player
item.description: The Giants team is flailing at the bottom of the ladder, winless after 11 rounds and the likely recipient of this year's wooden spoon. But their brand new debutant Eva Carneiro has brought some much needed excitement for fans, as the teenager breaks the record for the youngest-ever player in Super Netball.

item.title: Vandals saw off road signs in protest against reduced speed limits on Victorian roads
item.description: Police believe changes to speed limits on country roads in Victoria have led to vandalism including new road signs being cut down.

item.title: Vivid Sydney drone shows cancelled for now after malfunction saw 89 drones fall into the harbour
item.description: The incident occurred not long into Monday's 7:30pm "star-bound" show when almost 90 drones began falling out of formation and into the harbour around Cockle Bay.

item.title: Essendon part ways with Brad Scott as AFL head coach
item.description: Essendon sacks head coach Brad Scott as the Bombers languish at the bottom of the AFL ladder.

item.title: A century on from Miles Davis's birth, his legacy still shapes jazz
item.description: From Illinois to New York to Australia, legendary trumpeter Miles Davis helped shape the direction of jazz with albums like Birth of the Cool and Kind of Blue.

item.title: 'Inhumane' NDIS home in Broome subject to allegations of neglect
item.description: Former workers at a disability home in Western Australia's far north raise allegations of neglect, including medical mismanagement, locked fridges and a child being placed at risk.

item.title: Riley McGree to miss FIFA World Cup for Socceroos with injury
item.description: The Socceroos have been dealt a massive injury blow ahead of next month's FIFA World Cup, with midfielder Riley McGree ruled out of the tournament.

item.title: Mitchell Moses ruled out of State of Origin Game I for NSW Blues
item.description: The NSW Blues have confirmed five-eighth Mitchell Moses will miss Wednesday night's State of Origin opener due to a hamstring injury.

item.title: 'Morale' boost for Brokenwood Wines after 2026 NSW President's Medal win
item.description: A winery squeezing value out of every part of the vine has received the country's top agricultural gong at a difficult time for the wine industry.

item.title: Israeli Opposition Leader Yair Lapid calls for investigation into Ben-Gvir after video of Gaza flotilla activists
item.description: One of Israel's most prominent opposition politicians has called for an investigation into Israel's far-right national security minister, after he posted video showing alleged abuse of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla last week.

item.title: Rockhampton environmentalists call for action as supermarket trolleys pollute local waterways
item.description: A "tragedy of the trolleys" is unfolding in Rockhampton, where a lack of governance over waterways is creating an environmental hazard.

item.title: Shopping trolleys pollute creek in Rockhampton
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Grain growers adapt as low May rainfall highlights a drying autumn trend
item.description: A downward trend in autumn rainfall has WA farmers adapting to early seeding, but hoping this week's forecast cold front will provide a much-needed break to the dry spell.

item.title: Government sets up fight with second-largest gas exporter Santos over domestic reservation plan
item.description: The federal government's draft framework would require gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their exports to the domestic market, with limited exceptions, which gas producers warn would "undermine" future supply.

item.title: Sydney crime figure shot in Vietnam
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Two arrested in Vietnam over shooting death of Sydney gangster Lorenzo Lemalu
item.description: Authorities in Vietnam have arrested two men after Sydney crime figure Lorenzo Lemalu was shot dead in Ho Chi Minh City last week.

item.title: US Central Command says it has launched strikes against Iranian boats and missile launch sites
item.description: US Centcom says the strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".

item.title: Pipes from former church organ stolen in Adelaide's eastern suburbs, SA Police investigates
item.description: The pipe theft comes days after a bronze statue was stolen from the centre of a fountain in Adelaide's inner north-eastern suburbs.

item.title: SA Police return to Yunta as part of Gus Lamont investigation
item.description: Police have returned to Yunta in South Australia's far north as part of their investigation into the disappearance of four-year-old boy Gus Lamont.

item.title: Roger Tuivasa-Sheck to miss rest of 2026 NRL season after ACL injury
item.description: Warriors club legend Roger Tuivasa-Sheck appears to have played his last NRL game after confirming he suffered an anterior cruciate ligament injury in round 12.

item.title: NT diphtheria cases falling as 10,000 vaccines rolled out, test confirms man's death related
item.description: The Northern Territory government says the rate of new diphtheria cases in the territory has fallen from more than 20 per week to less than 10 after 10,000 Territorians stepped up to be immunised against the ongoing outbreak.

item.title: Driver dies in head-on crash with road train that forced Stuart Highway closure
item.description: A man from the Northern Territory has been killed in a head-on crash with a truck that forced the closure of the Stuart Highway in South Australia's far north earlier today.

item.title: When moving forward means being the cycle-breaker in a family
item.description: Motherhood has a way of making family patterns impossible to ignore. Suddenly you start asking questions you never asked before.

item.title: Teen who robbed tourists after escaping detention centre avoids further time behind bars
item.description: One of the teenagers involved in a group escape from the Ashley Youth Detention Centre in December is sentenced for his involvement in the armed robbery of two tourists.

item.title: Why rugby league needs State of Origin to save a lost NRL season
item.description: Players, coaches and fans are disillusioned with the rule changes that have come to define this NRL season but ahead of Origin I can the interstate series put a broken season back together?

item.title: Sonny Rollins, saxophonist and restless genius of jazz, dies at 95
item.description: Rollins, one of the greatest saxophonists of all time, played with everyone from Miles Davis to The Rolling Stones.

item.title: Brinkworth family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for Blackford bushfire
item.description: A court judgement that found pastoralist Tom Brinkworth liable for a 2021 bushfire, five months after he died, has been upheld after an appeal by Mr Brinkworth's family.

item.title: What are Trump's Abraham Accords, and will other countries join?
item.description: As US President Donald Trump pushes for a deal with Iran, he is reviving one of the signature foreign policy projects of his first term: the Abraham Accords.

item.title: Victoria Police taskforce investigating Dezi Freeman arrests two men
item.description: Detectives investigating the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman have arrested two men in Victoria's north-east.

item.title: Kumanjayi White family 'heartbroken' no charges laid over custody death
item.description: The family of Kumanjayi White tell the ABC they are "heartbroken" that no charges will be laid over his death in custody, as Northern Territory Police confirm their criminal investigation has concluded.

item.title: Curtis Mead hits two home runs in MLB meeting, but man in Australian jersey steals show
item.description: Curtis Mead hit two home runs for Washington against fellow Australian Travis Bazzana's Cleveland side as two Australian-born players faced off in an MLB game for the first time since 2017. But it was a man in an Australian supporters shirt who stole the show.

item.title: BHP dumped plans for processing plant that would cut global emissions by 1.7 million tonnes a year
item.description: Internal BHP documents reveal how the mining giant planned to set up an iron ore processing facility in the Pilbara to cut carbon emissions before backing away from it entirely.

item.title: With 'advantage' shifting to Ukraine, Russia hits Kyiv with Oreshnik hypersonic missile
item.description: Russia escalates attacks on Kyiv by using a hypersonic missile in one of its largest assaults on the Ukrainian capital since the start of the four-year war, and warns foreigners to leave the city ahead of further strikes.

item.title: Shoko Kawata to become first mayor in Japan to take maternity leave while in office
item.description: A Japanese mayor will take maternity leave this year, becoming the first in the country's history to do so while in office.

item.title: Court orders developer Theo Onisforou not to contact Wingecarribee mayor, general manager
item.description: A Southern Highlands property developer is only allowed to communicate with his local council's mayor and general manager through his lawyer under interim AVOs issued against him during a heated court appearance.

item.title: Navy chief says Chinese flotilla in Tasman Sea stirred anxiety and anger in Australia, NZ
item.description: Australia's Chief of Navy says China stirred "anger" in New Zealand when it sent a powerful naval flotilla into the Tasman Sea last year, encouraging political support on both sides of the ditch for increased defence spending.

item.title: Police officer accused of rape sent message denying he was 'sleazy', court told
item.description: A police officer goes on trial in South Australia's District Court after pleading not guilty to two counts of rape and one count of attempted rape.

item.title: Iluwanti Ken highlights mistreatment of APY Art Centre Collective after 'white hands on black art'
item.description: Award-winning artist Iluwanti Ken says the withdrawal of funding and support over the "white hands on black art" scandal has left her feeling like a stranger in her own industry.

item.title: Ricky Hampson discovered he was a Stolen Generations survivor. Now, he's calling for action
item.description: Ricky Hampson spent most of his life believing he had been adopted before learning several years ago he is a survivor of the Stolen Generations. Today, he is calling for more action and truth-telling.

item.title: New documents reveal Bondi gunman Naveed Akram remained on ASIO and police radar in 2022
item.description: Naveed Akram and his father travelled to Uzbekistan around 2022 and a former terror hotspot in the Philippines shortly before committing the Bondi massacre.

item.title: Romanian man jailed for 'distraction fraud' of elderly Australian shoppers
item.description: The Romanian citizen, who illegally visited Australia as part of a global "distraction fraud" operation, has been jailed after fleecing thousands of dollars from elderly Australian shoppers.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Anti-corruption commissioner Paul Brereton admits being a 'distraction', contributing to own 'downfall'
item.description: The corruption watchdog boss, appearing during a tense grilling at Senate estimates, admits his conduct became a "distraction".

item.title: Commonwealth Bank CEO: AI will bring a transition the workplace
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Remembering AFL great Neale Daniher
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Commonwealth Bank boss urges government to rethink CGT changes
item.description: The Commonwealth Bank boss speaks out about the government's tax changes.

item.title: At least 11 dead as Europe bakes in unprecedented heatwave
item.description: The British capital's previous hottest May day was set only one day prior, when the mercury topped 33.5C.

item.title: Report warns AI data centre boom threatens Australia's energy transition
item.description: A new report concludes the AI-fuelled surge of power-hungry data centres across Australia is jeopardising the country's energy transition.

item.title: State of Origin: Tackle our More or Less quiz ahead of Game I
item.description: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

item.title: New Blak Douglas work to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art
item.description: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

item.title: Iran war derailed talks to claw back billions in tax credits paid to miners
item.description: Billions in tax credits are paid to coal miners every year. Ministers discussed clawing back some of that money in the May federal budget, but the Iran war has pushed out those talks.

item.title: Woman battles GIO Suncorp as mould grows in Sydney home after roof repairs
item.description: Lily lodged an insurance claim with her provider GIO Suncorp after a truck ran through her street's power lines. Nearly a decade later, she has been forced out of her home.

item.title: School students have grown up online but test shows digital literacy at new low
item.description: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.

item.title: What time is State of Origin Game I? How to watch and everything you need to know about the Blues and Maroons
item.description: The fiercest rival in Australian rugby league resumes tonight, as New South Wales and Queensland meet in the opening match of the 2026 men's State of Origin series.

item.title: Wallabies facing same selection headache as State of Origin's NSW Blues
item.description: Just as NSW Blues coach Laurie Daley had a difficult selection decision to make, Joe Schmidt may be thinking long and hard about one position in the Wallabies' back line for July's Nations Championship Tests.

item.title: Brad Scott sacking a chance for Essendon to step out of James Hird's shadow
item.description: Essendon finds itself at a clear demarcation point after sacking Brad Scott as the shadow of James Hird once again looms large over the troubled club.

item.title: Finding out you are adopted or donor-conceived after taking an ancestry DNA test
item.description: Direct-to-consumer genetic testing kits are often given as gifts or purchased "as a bit of fun", which means people often aren't prepared to discover unexpected parents, siblings and other relatives.

item.title: BHP is rightly sensitive about its reputation as a corporate leader on climate change
item.description: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

item.title: Attorney-General's Department contacted Australian Cyber Security Centre when notified of court privacy breach
item.description: Litigants in at least 146 court matters were potentially involved in a data breach that is now the subject of a formal complaint with the privacy commissioner in Canada, Senate estimates has been told.

item.title: Reception hours slashed at police stations in Melbourne's outer-north
item.description: Victoria Police says its decision to cut the hours police stations are open to the public in Melbourne's outer north will result in more police on the frontline.

item.title: How did almost 90 drones crash during Sydney's Vivid festival?
item.description: The company behind the drone shows at Sydney's Vivid festival said spectators were not at risk when almost 90 devices fell from the sky during a display on Monday night.

item.title: SAPOL commissioner rejects incentive plan to attract officers to 'understaffed' outback town
item.description: Commissioner Grant Stevens rejects recommendations to attract more officers to the understaffed Coober Pedy station over funding concerns.

item.title: Hungry eleven-armed seastars making a meal of Tasmanian scallops
item.description: The fast spread of pest eleven-armed seastars has fishers, along with researchers, concerned.

item.title: Fidel Castro's daughter fled his regime. Now, she hopes the US can bring it down
item.description: Fidel Castro's daughter says the US has failed to find a way to deal with Cuba for decades. But she tells the ABC that the Trump administration has a difference that could spell the end of the regime she fled.

item.title: Drug-linked murderer Garry Thompson returned to jail for breaching parole
item.description: Murderer Garry Thompson has been returned to custody for 12 months for breaching his parole conditions multiple times.

item.title: Wiradjuri community devastated by destruction of rock shelter by ACEREZ
item.description: Members of a First Nations community in Central West NSW say they were devastated to learn a culturally significant rock shelter had been destroyed to make way for a renewable energy project.

item.title: Rare sea turtle hybrid hatches at Queensland's largest mainland turtle nesting site
item.description: Rare baby turtles that are a cross between the loggerhead and green species have been found on the beach at Mon Repos.

item.title: A hybrid turtle hatches at Mon Repos.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iran calls latest US strikes 'bad faith' sign as internet is slowly restored
item.description: Iran has denounced US strikes in the country's south as a sign of "bad faith and unreliability" as negotiations toward a possible deal to end the war continue.

item.title: State of Origin star Cameron Munster's Maroons triumph anniversary brings heartache
item.description: Cameron Munster's mum reveals the heartbreaking call she had to make before her superstar son led Queensland to victory days after the shock death of his dad.

item.title: M Resources and Jindal Steel announced as two remaining Whyalla steelworks bidders
item.description: Peter Malinauskas has announced M Resources and Jindal Steel are the two bidders remaining for the financially stricken Whyalla steelworks, while BlueScope Steel retains its right of last offer.

item.title: NSW concern about cost of monitoring IS-linked women and children — as it happened
item.description: The boss of home affairs says the NSW government raised concerns about the cost of monitoring IS-linked women and children who recently returned to the country from Syria.

item.title: Peer support worker Brandie McGrady is a Dubbo Drug Court success story
item.description: The peer support worker at Dubbo's Bunmabunmarra service knows firsthand how the drug court can offer a second chance.

item.title: Australian Adam Walton stuns former world number one Daniil Medvedev at French Open
item.description: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

item.title: Brad Scott reveals he wanted Essendon to trade Zach Merrett, confirms bumper Harley Reid offer
item.description: The ousted Essendon coach reveals he was against the decision to keep Zach Merrett at the club despite a late trade offer from Hawthorn.

item.title: Rain brings potential 'opportunities' in renewed search for Gus Lamont, police say
item.description: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.

item.title: Senior student says Morawa Ag College overrun by mice before toxic bait closure
item.description: A year 12 student at the WA College of Agriculture Morawa says there were fears of "cooking" mice in the heating system before the mistaken deployment of toxic baits shut the school down.

item.title: ASX jumps as odds of June rate rise move close to zero — as it happened
item.description: Shares climbed on the ASX after softer than expected inflation figures sent market bets of a June rate rise to less than 10 per cent.

item.title: NSW coach Laurie Daley says Nathan Cleary unfairly judged over losing State of Origin record
item.description: NSW coach Laurie Daley says the responsibility does not solely rest on the shoulders of his star No.7 ahead of this year's State of Origin campaign.

item.title: BP removes chair Albert Manifold over 'serious conduct and governance concerns'
item.description: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and "overbearing" behaviour, which he later described as a "false narrative".

item.title: St John Ambulance NT considering body-worn cameras amid ongoing assaults on paramedics
item.description: The Northern Territory's ambulance service is considering introducing police-style body-worn cameras after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis led to rioting in Alice Springs last month.

item.title: Government pitches largest overhaul to unemployment system in decades
item.description: Jobseeker services will shift away from a one-size-fits-all approach to a new tiered model, to be unveiled by the government later today.

item.title: Trailblazing female driving instructor's extraordinary legacy in South Australia's Riverland
item.description: One of South Australia's first female licensed driving instructors has been remembered for her tireless advocacy and devotion to her community.

item.title: Rescuers explore narrow Laos cave to save trapped people
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Two men released without charge after Dezi Freeman-related arrest
item.description: A 48-year-old and a 45-year-old were taken into custody by detectives from Taskforce Summit, who continued to investigate Freeman's movements after the fatal shooting of officers Neal Thompson and Vadim de Waart-Hottart at a Porepunkah property in Victoria's north-east in August last year.

item.title: Australia's Kimberly Birrell stuns Jessica Pegula as Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka dazzle at French Open
item.description: Australia's Kimberly Birrell continues a day of upsets for Australia by advancing to the second round of the French Open for the first time in her career.

item.title: Alexia Putellas ends 14-season Barcelona career after fourth Champions League title
item.description: Alexia Putellas ends her stint at Barcelona after winning 10 league titles and four Champions Leagues with the Spanish giants.

item.title: War crimes investigators refer media leaks on Ben Roberts-Smith arrest to corruption commission
item.description: News crews were at Sydney Airport as a plane carrying Ben Roberts-Smith landed, and captured images of his arrest from inside the terminal.

item.title: Scholarship to be created in memory of farmer Callum Smail after death at 26
item.description: A scholarship will be created at a Victorian agricultural college in memory of a young man who died in a farming accident last month.

item.title: Consortium headed by Adroit Capital announced as preferred bidder for Liberty Ball Bay
item.description: A consortium headed by Adroit Capital has been announced as the preferred bidder for the smelter in northern Tasmania, as federal and state government pledge another $5 million to support workers in the weeks ahead.

item.title: Thousands of Muslims pray on Mount Arafat for 2026 Hajj pilgrimage
item.description: Thousands of Muslim worshippers converge on the rocky hills and plains of Saudi Arabia's Mount Arafat as the annual Islamic pilgrimage, the Hajj, reached its peak.

item.title: SA Police domestic violence operation results in 70 arrests across state in April
item.description: SA Police say they targeted 351 offenders as part of Operation Storm Days of Action, attending 443 addresses across the state in April.

item.title: NASA has revealed its plan to live on the Moon. Here's how it would work
item.description: The US space agency has outlined plans for a sprawling lunar outpost involving rovers, hopping drones and resource-extraction technology as part of a renewed race to the Moon.

item.title: Lord Howe Island invertebrates boom after rodent eradication
item.description: Researchers say the removal of invasive rodents on Lord Howe Island has sparked a rebound of invertebrate life, including native cockroaches and other insects, vital to the broader ecosystem.

item.title: Concussion saw 15-year-old Macy Watson lose her 'spark', but a new trial helped her recover
item.description: Macy became a shell of her usual self after suffering a concussion on a school trip, but experienced a huge improvement after a trial designed to help children.

item.title: Sex worker killer Xiaozheng Lin has jail sentence increased following appeal
item.description: Xiaozheng Lin will spend more time in prison for killing two sex workers in 2022, following an appeal by the prosecutor.

item.title: Since placing his first bet at age 15, Dylan says he's lost at least $900k
item.description: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teens gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.

item.title: BOM plans to build new radar at Tennant Creek airport, 11 years after closure
item.description: BOM says it has decided to install a new weather radar in Tennant Creek, in the same place as the previous radar.

item.title: Vladimir Putin launches new incentives to recruit more soldiers for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: The Kremlin is trying to recruit more soldiers for its war in Ukraine but an analyst says at the rate of casualties, "Russia cannot replace recruits fast enough to keep the 'meat grinder' fed."

item.title: Allegedly stolen cars cause peak hour traffic chaos in Cairns
item.description: Police have arrested four teenage boys after three allegedly stolen cars caused traffic chaos across several Cairns suburbs today.

item.title: Cuts of meat that can save you money and how to cook with them
item.description: If you are looking to trim your household food budget, lower-cost cuts of meat could be a good place to start. Here are some tips on what to look for and how to cook them.

item.title: Adelaide track designer estimates MotoGP remodel could cost more than $100m
item.description: Adelaide Street Circuit designer Bob Barnard estimates it could cost more than $100 million to remodel the track for the MotoGP, but the premier says that number has not been mentioned to him.

item.title: Alleged Bathurst bomb maker charged with endangering someone's life
item.description: A man has been charged with more than a dozen further explosives offences related to an alleged bomb-making lab at his home in a small NSW village.

item.title: The government's proposed changes to the unemployment system, explained
item.description: The federal government has proposed changes to Australia's $2 billion employment services system to make them “more responsive, effective and dignified”.

item.title: Measles cases rise in Australia as authorities monitor deadly outbreaks in South and South-East Asia
item.description: Authorities keep a close eye on measles outbreaks in Asia, as many cases reported in Australia continue to be returned overseas travellers.

item.title: Councils responsible for more than 'rates, roads and rubbish' as Geraldton LGA buys land from state
item.description: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.

item.title: Bendigo teaching student charged with offences relating to online child abuse material
item.description: A Victorian teaching student, who had recently worked as a childcare centre cook, a foster carer and was a school council president, is facing charges relating to online child abuse material.

item.title: Woman changing tyre assaulted in alleged kidnap attempt in Sydney, police say
item.description: A 27-year-old woman was changing a tyre when a man allegedly threatened her with a knife and attempted to drag her into an SUV, NSW Police said.

item.title: Two allegedly stolen cars on Mulgrave Road in Cairns
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pentagon denies reports of clash with SpaceX over Starlink price hike during Iran war
item.description: The Pentagon has denied claims that the department has been clashing with Elon Musk's SpaceX over costs to use its satellite internet network, which the military's kamikaze drones rely upon in the war against Iran.

item.title: Jim Chalmers flags 'legitimate' small business capital gains tax debate
item.description: The government is treading carefully on carve-outs to its capital gains tax legislation amid backlash to its plan.

item.title: Prem Kumar to stand trial over alleged murder of Anu Eugene found dead in Cobram home
item.description: A man charged over the murder of a woman who was found dead in a Northern Victorian home has been committed to stand trial over her death.

item.title: Accused 'money mules' refused bail in ACT court after allegedly moving scam proceeds
item.description: Police say so-called "money mules" facilitate the swift movement of fraudulently obtained money.

item.title: Welcome rain for western NSW
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Drought-affected farmers welcome significant rainfall but some miss out
item.description: Up to 100 millimetres of rain has been welcomed across drought-hit parts of western NSW, but some farmers expecting relief were left disappointed.

item.title: No 'plan B' for submarines if AUKUS fails, says Defence Minister Richard Marles
item.description: The defence minister brushes off calls for a "plan B" in case AUKUS fails, declaring it would be a step towards "giving up" on acquiring any new submarines at all.

item.title: Federal Attorney-General warns NT crime agenda could breach children's human rights
item.description: Federal Attorney-General Michelle Rowland warns her Northern Territory counterpart that the NT's tough-on-crime agenda could breach Australia's international human rights obligations.

item.title: NSW government approves Copi Mineral Sands Project near Victorian border town of Mildura
item.description: A mineral sands project of global strategic importance receives provisional approval from the New South Wales government.

item.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight
item.description: An Australian woman with links to ISIS, who is barred from Australia on a temporary exclusion order, tried to board a flight home with the other members of Tuesday's returning cohort, a Senate estimates hearing has been told.

item.title: Maugean skates released back into Macquarie Harbour after two years in captivity
item.description: A pair of endangered rays taken from a Tasmanian harbour as a species "insurance policy" have been released back into the wild after helping to produce more than 400 eggs for a captive research program.

item.title: Albany bakery cops fines over $60,000 after inspectors find rodent faeces and animal blood on bread
item.description: Inspectors found animal blood dripping onto bread, rodent faeces on food and spreading mould at the Albany French Hot Bread Shop, triggering prosecution and $60,000 in penalties for the owners.

item.title: Congo national Christian Mpongo sentenced to prison after failing to help crash victims in fatal hit-and-run
item.description: The family of a man who was left to die alongside his severely injured brother and father after a hit-and-run crash, say they will appeal the "light" sentence handed to the man who was behind the wheel.

item.title: Labor MP who unseated Peter Dutton defends electoral commission referral over empty block
item.description: Dickson MP Ali France has been referred to the Australian Electoral Commission with claims she is registered to vote at a vacant suburban block in her north Brisbane electorate.

item.title: Maroons let 20-0 lead slip in Origin I after Kalyn Ponga sent off late
item.description: James Tedesco scores a spectacular try with 90 seconds left in State of Origin I, capping off a 20-point comeback after Queensland fullback Kalyn Ponga is sent off for a shoulder charge.

item.title: NT child protection bill consultation was 'limited' due to 'time constraints', department says
item.description: Despite taking a year to prepare a controversial bill surrounding child protection, the NT Department of Children and Families says consultation with organisations was "limited".

item.title: Whyalla locals call for more certainty around steelworks' future amid news of final bidders
item.description: Whyalla locals welcome more certainty about the future ownership of the city's steelworks, but say it is still not enough to warrant business investment and workers coming back to the Spencer Gulf.

item.title: Man dead and two injured after alleged assaults at Childers and North Isis
item.description: One man is dead and two others are in hospital, as police declare multiple crime scenes in a regional Queensland town.

item.title: Inquest hears NDIS provider delayed suicide threat reports before teen's death
item.description: The inquest in Southport is examining whether any deficiencies in the boy's care or accommodation caused or contributed to his death.

item.title: 'Optimism' as Walk for Truth ends despite no formal commitment from PM
item.description: The Walk for Truth ended at Parliament House today and while there was not a firm commitment from the PM, many felt the response was a 'positive move'.

item.title: Death in custody of Patience Kuzmins leaves family heartbroken, inquest hears
item.description: The family of a young Indigenous woman who died in a NSW jail has told an inquest that she was a talented artist who generously supported anyone in need. The inquest is examining Corrective Services NSW's search and body scan practices.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: NT police say cause of Kumanjayi White's death in custody in Alice Springs still unknown
item.description: The Northern Territory police commissioner says the cause of 24-year-old Kumanjayi White's death in custody remains undetermined, with the criminal case against the officers who restrained him now closed.

item.title: Police vision of allegedly stolen cars in Cairns
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NASA reveals plans for living on the moon
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Five people trapped in flooded cave in Laos found alive but two still missing
item.description: Five of the seven people, who entered the cave in central Laos more than a week ago, have been found alive. Rescuers are still searching for two.

item.title: State of Origin quick hits: James Tedesco scores Blues' match-winner, Maroons star Kalyn Ponga sent off
item.description: The Blues leave it late, the Maroons finish with 12 players, while the bunker draws the ire of players, commentators and spectators alike. Here are five quick hits from State of Origin I.

item.title: State of Origin: Kalyn Ponga shattered by send-off in Game I as Ashley Klein overrules bunker
item.description: Kalyn Ponga is left feeling responsible for Queensland's Origin I collapse as the decision to send him off leaves several greats shocked.

item.title: Trump says US 'not satisfied' with Iran peace deal proposals
item.description: It comes as the White House rejects a draft deal being reported by Iranian state TV as a "fabrication".

item.title: UK spy chief says West between peace and war and 500,000 Russians killed in Ukraine war so far
item.description: The head of a major UK intelligence agency is warning that Moscow and other adversaries could use artificial intelligence and other emerging technology to conduct "hybrid warfare" against Western allies.

item.title: New York and New Jersey investigate 2026 FIFA World Cup ticket prices
item.description: In a joint statement, New Jersey's attorney-general accused the football governing body of having turned "buying a ticket to the World Cup into a gauntlet of confusion, fake scarcity, and impossibly high prices".

item.title: Explain the Internet: The Backrooms captured cyberspace and now it's infecting cinemas
item.description: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

item.title: Put your Australian TV show knowledge to the test with this picture quiz
item.description: How well do you know your Neighbours from your Deadloch? Take our single-frame Australian TV quiz to find out.

item.title: Israel warns all residents in southern Lebanon to leave
item.description: It is the first time Israel has made such a sweeping demand since US President Donald Trump announced in April a ceasefire had been imposed in Lebanon.

item.title: Labor is quietly confident the battery boom will help prevent power price shocks
item.description: Within the government, there's quiet confidence the battery boom will continue to gather pace and help shield Australia from both global shocks and domestic demand surges, driving prices even lower.

item.title: Australia ranks third in the world for battery capacity and WA is leading the charge
item.description: Western Australia in particular is proving to the world that grid-scale batteries are no longer a complementary technology but can play a central role in power grids.

item.title: Iran's internet is coming back online. But it's not back to normal
item.description: Reports of an increase in internet traffic are filtering through, but experts say Iran has a long way to go to get back up to speed.

item.title: The world is matching Australia's social media ban. The UK is set to join the crackdown
item.description: The UK could become the latest nation to implement a social media ban, after British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vowed to introduce a "game changer" policy.

item.title: Fears Melbourne man who disappeared during India trip may have met with foul play
item.description: The family of maths teacher Sunil Sharma say the 66-year-old disappeared nearly a week ago, while trying to sell a property in the Punjabi city of Amritsar.

item.title: State of Origin: Ethan Strange and James Tedesco star in Blues' 22-20 win over Maroons
item.description: When the Blues needed heroes to get on top of the Maroons in State of Origin I, they turned to a debutante and the oldest player on the field.

item.title: Torquay artist's willie wagtail paintings taking flight around the Surf Coast and beyond
item.description: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.

item.title: Labor's capital gains tax challenge deepens as biotech sector raises alarm
item.description: Another sector has called for a capital gains tax change carve-out, warning the government that it will face a talent drain without it.

item.title: Upper house member Ruth Forrest calls for apology after personal health request leaked to media
item.description: A Tasmanian independent politician is calling for an apology after her personal health-related request via email to the government was leaked to a media outlet.

item.title: One in 10 Australian teens relying on withdrawal method and half not using condoms as STIs rise
item.description: A long-running survey of Australian teens has identified falling condom use and unwanted sex as major concerns for young people starting relationships.

item.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for role in Friends actor's death
item.description: Matthew Perry's live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the Friends star's descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with the fatal dose of the drug, has been sentenced to three years and five months in prison.

item.title: Can farmers become less reliant on synthetic fertiliser? Some are trying
item.description: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.

item.title: Water for 52 Olympic-sized pools lost daily in Queensland due to pipe issues, report finds
item.description: Queensland councils are calling for more state funding to update the state's pipe network, which they say has deteriorated to a high-risk condition.

item.title: Regional employers, academics say one-size-fits-all migration policy will decimate critical workforces
item.description: Amid ongoing discussion about Australia's immigration intake and migration policies, regional residents say many industries in the bush cannot survive without overseas workers.

item.title: Load limits on Victorian bridges 'wreaking havoc' as councils stuggle to fund maintenance
item.description: Councils across regional Victoria say they are being forced to ban trucks from some bridges because they cannot afford to fix them to keep them safe.

item.title: Brunswick bike shop burns down as arsonists attack Keysborough car parts store
item.description: Firefighters responded to separate blazes in Melbourne overnight, with police investigating one as an arson attack.

item.title: Teenager stabbed at Highpoint Shopping Centre during fight between youths in Melbourne's west
item.description: The fight broke out in the dining and entertainment precinct of Highpoint Shopping Centre in Maribyrnong.

item.title: Brothers reunited after being separated as part of Stolen Generation
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Stolen Generations brothers share their story ahead of Reconciliation Week
item.description: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

item.title: Rain animation
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wind animation
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rainy weather drenching Australia's east could turn May into one of the wettest on record
item.description: A series of low-pressure systems are drenching Australia this week, quickly turning this May into one of the wettest on record for parts of the country's east.

item.title: 'Second wave' of algal bloom forecast for SA's Spencer Gulf
item.description: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

item.title: Man avoids jail after indecently abusing woman he met on Tinder in Canberra
item.description: The ACT Supreme Court heard the man was concerned only with his own sexual gratification when he abused his victim.

item.title: Legal action filed against federal environment minister's decision to allow land clearing at NT station
item.description: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

item.title: Live: Minister brushes aside calls from WA premier for CGT carve-outs for mineral exploration
item.description: The federal government will introduce legislation to parliament today to overhaul negative gearing and the capital gains tax. Follow live.

item.title: WA teachers set to be trained to better support children with higher needs
item.description: The WA government has committed $16 million for a professional learning hub that will include mandatory inductions for special needs education assistants and disability modules for graduate teachers.

item.title: Live: ASX set top open lower as price of oil drops
item.description: The Aussie share market is set to open lower as the price of oil drops. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Man charged with murder after multiple alleged assaults in Childers
item.description: A 36-year-old man has been charged with murder after allegedly attacking several people south of Bundaberg.

item.title: Police charge five teens over alleged stolen car rampage in Cairns
item.description: After chaotic scenes in Cairns yesterday morning, police say they have arrested five teenagers.

item.title: Billy Slater refuses to guarantee Reece Walsh State of Origin recall despite Queensland's collapse
item.description: Queensland's coach bats away questions about the notable absence of Brisbane's star fullback after seeing his side cough up a 20-point lead.

item.title: Stagnant biofuel industry looks to expand on Western Australia's south coast
item.description: In the 1990s, Esperance was seen as a key to a major biofuels industry for Western Australia. Thirty years later, the industry has seen little change. But some say the area has untapped potential as a major fuel supplier.

item.title: Live: ABC experts take your questions about Iran war as it enters fourth month
item.description: ABC experts are taking questions abut the Iran war as the conflict enters its fourth month with no clear end in sight. Follow live.

item.title: French Open: Alejandro Davidovich Fokina's coach quits via text as Novak Djokovic survives stiff test
item.description: Alenajdro Davidovich Fokina's French Open comes to an end shortly after his coach flees to Miami after claiming he was unwell after a lunch catch-up.

item.title: Not All Clubs calls for a stand against gender-based violence with Wear Red round
item.description: Not All Clubs calls out inequality in sporting organisations across the country. This weekend, the group is calling on teams to wear red armbands in memory of the women whose lives have been claimed by gender-based violence this year.
